product_summary.one_sentence_summary: Medical French training concept for nurses targeting French-speaking healthcare systems where language proof and recognition rules differ by country.
product_summary.target_user: International nurses and staffing partners targeting France, Belgium and Switzerland.
product_summary.core_pain: Nurses need job-ready medical French and still have to clear country-specific recognition and language-proof requirements.
product_summary.claimed_outcome: Better medical French and a smoother path toward employment in French-speaking healthcare systems.

primary_buyer_type.reason: The offer can be sold directly to nurses or through staffing/employer channels, so the motion is hybrid.

secondary_buyer_types.reason: Recruitment partners may want language preparation as part of a placement pipeline.
Hospitals and care groups can buy language preparation to improve hiring readiness.
Nurses themselves are still the end users and may buy the course directly.

budget_owner.reason: Budget may sit with the learner, an agency or the hiring employer depending on the go-to-market route.

buyer_influencers.reason: Recognition, visa and language-proof rules shape whether the course is useful.
Recruitment and onboarding teams care about route readiness.
Clinical managers care whether the nurse can function in a French-speaking workplace.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: France requires recognition or authorization to exercise for foreign-trained paramedical professions.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Service-Public states that regulated paramedical professions require the corresponding diploma or authorization to exercise in France.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Belgium requires foreign-trained healthcare professionals to obtain a visa and prove sufficient language knowledge; B2 applies to nurses at bachelor's level.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: Belgian FPS Public Health states that nurses need a visa and that B2 is the required language level for the relevant bachelor's-level healthcare professions.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: Switzerland requires foreign medical and psychology qualifications to be submitted in a national language.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The Swiss FOPH states that applications must be submitted in German, French or Italian.

externally_checkable_claims.claim_text: No dedicated online Medical French provider identified.
externally_checkable_claims.notes: The reviewed public sources show strong substitutes and general language providers, but not an exhaustive market census of online medical French offers.

internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: No primary official source reviewed confirmed this figure in the context of this product.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The same model can be applied to France, Belgium and Switzerland without material redesign.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The public evidence shows the countries have different recognition and language rules.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The product's certificate will be accepted for practice or recognition.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: Acceptance depends on the destination authority, not on the provider's course label.

internal_claims_unverified.claim_text: The market lacks a dedicated online Medical French alternative.
internal_claims_unverified.reason_unverified: The reviewed sources do not provide an exhaustive market census.

regulator_entities.name: Centre ENIC-NARIC France
regulator_entities.relevance: Provides comparability recognition for foreign diplomas in France.

regulator_entities.name: FPS Public Health (Belgium)
regulator_entities.relevance: Issues visa and language-proof guidance for healthcare professions in Belgium.

regulator_entities.name: Federal Office of Public Health (FOPH) / MEBEKO
regulator_entities.relevance: Evaluates recognition of foreign medical diplomas in Switzerland.

framework_entities.name: Directive 2005/36/EC
framework_entities.relevance: EU professional-qualification recognition framework relevant to cross-border nursing.

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Foreign-trained paramedical professionals need recognition or authorization to exercise in France.
regulatory_requirements.applies_to: Foreign-trained nurses and related regulated professions

regulatory_requirements.requirement: Foreign-trained healthcare professionals need a visa to practise in Belgium, and B2 language proof applies to nurses at bachelor's level.
Foreign medical diplomas in Switzerland must be submitted in German, French or Italian.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: Prolingua
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Offers tailor-made company language courses and specialist language options that can substitute for some of this need.

competitors_or_substitutes.name: inlingua Luxembourg
competitors_or_substitutes.notes: Offers French courses for adults and companies with online and in-person delivery.

legal_claim_severity.reason: The offer is directly adjacent to regulated-profession recognition and accepted language-proof claims.

authority_needed_for_delivery.reason: Credible delivery requires medical-language and route expertise beyond generic French teaching.

authority_needed_for_claims.reason: Claims about recognition, visa and accepted proof depend on external authorities, not the provider.

proof_stack.reason: The product needs to show that it moves candidates toward accepted language proof, not just classroom progress.
A route to acceptance by employers or authorities matters more than a generic participation certificate.
The end value is working in a French-speaking healthcare system.
Buyers need to prefer this niche route over generic French training or employer-led alternatives.

proof_burden.reason: Route acceptance and placement outcomes are the real proof standard here.

dependency_types.reason: Recognition and exercise authorization are part of the offer's value chain.
Destination-country authorities decide what counts as accepted proof.
Recruitment partners are likely needed to make the pipeline commercially useful.
Medical French plus recognition guidance benefits from specialist healthcare and migration input.
France, Belgium and Switzerland differ materially in rules and accepted proof.
Specialist medical French requires teachers who can handle healthcare vocabulary and route context.

scope_variance_risk.reason: The current scope spans three jurisdictions with materially different recognition and language rules.

expansion_variance_risk.reason: Expanding to new countries or professions would add more route complexity and proof differences.

subsidy_exists.reason: No direct subsidy route was confirmed for this specific product.

subsidy_eligibility_status.reason: Public support may exist in some systems, but this course structure is not linked to a confirmed subsidy route.

subsidy_conflict_flag.reason: No direct subsidy claim was confirmed, so no subsidy conflict is established.

market_regulatory_context.reason: The product sits directly beside healthcare practice, recognition and visa rules.

claim_language_risk.reason: Recognition, visa and accepted-proof wording can easily overstate what a course itself can deliver.

authority_dependency_risk.reason: The product depends on decisions made by destination-country authorities.

competitive_gap_risk.reason: Generic and tailored French substitutes already exist, so the market is not empty.

numeric_claim_risk.reason: The card's France healthcare-worker count is not confirmed by a primary source.

subsidy_conflict_risk.reason: No direct subsidy claim was confirmed for this product.

positioning_risk.reason: The offer can easily become a generic French course unless route-critical outcomes are made explicit.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: The same model can be applied to France, Belgium and Switzerland without material redesign.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The public sources show materially different recognition and language requirements in each country, so a single model is not obviously portable.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: The product's certificate will be accepted for practice or recognition.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: Acceptance depends on the destination authority; provider-issued certificates are not automatically valid.

claim_conflicts.claim_text: No dedicated online Medical French provider identified.
claim_conflicts.what_is_wrong: The review found substitutes, but not an exhaustive market census, so the absence claim is not proven.

recommended_manual_review.reason: Split the offer by destination country if you want credible claims.
Do not imply that a course certificate is accepted by destination authorities unless that is validated.
Choose whether the first wedge is France, Belgium or Switzerland instead of all three at once.
